google.note: Best platform for HVAC. High-intent 'near me' searches drive immediate leads.

meta.note: Strong for retargeting and seasonal promotions. Lead forms work well for AC tune-ups and maintenance plans.

linkedin.note: Not recommended. HVAC is a local consumer service — LinkedIn users aren't searching for AC repair.

lsa.note: Ideal LSA fit. HVAC emergency and service calls convert at high rates with Google Verified badge. Proximity-based targeting reaches nearby homeowners in need.

google.note: Top platform. Emergency plumbing searches ('burst pipe', 'water heater leaking') convert immediately.

meta.note: Decent for retargeting and water heater replacement promos. Not ideal for emergency services.

linkedin.note: Not recommended for residential plumbing. Commercial plumbing contractors may see some B2B value.

lsa.note: Ideal LSA fit. Emergency plumbing leads are perfectly suited for pay-per-lead. Proximity targeting ensures relevant local leads.

google.note: Excellent ROI. 'Roof replacement' and storm damage keywords drive high-value leads.

meta.note: Great for storm damage awareness campaigns and before/after showcase ads. Strong visual platform for roofing.

linkedin.note: Not recommended for residential roofing.

lsa.note: Ideal LSA fit. High job values make LSA's flat CPL very cost-effective. Storm damage leads convert quickly.

google.note: High-intent searches drive strong leads. Emergency electrical work converts well.

meta.note: Works for panel upgrades, EV charger installations, and generator promos.

linkedin.note: Not recommended for residential electrical services.

lsa.note: Ideal LSA fit. Emergency electrical work and panel upgrades convert well through LSA. Google Verified badge builds trust.

google.note: Best platform. Septic emergencies drive immediate high-intent searches.

meta.note: Limited value. Septic services are urgency-driven and don't perform well as social ads.

linkedin.note: Not recommended.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. Septic services may be LSA-eligible in some markets. Emergency calls benefit from proximity targeting.

google.note: Excellent for pest emergencies and seasonal pest control. High search volume.

meta.note: Great for seasonal awareness campaigns ('mosquito season is here') and recurring service plans.

linkedin.note: Not recommended for residential pest control.

lsa.note: Strong LSA fit. Affordable CPL and recurring service potential. Seasonal pest leads convert well with Google Verified badge.

google.note: Strong seasonal performance. Pool building and maintenance keywords convert well.

meta.note: Excellent visual platform — pool builds, before/after photos perform very well. Great for seasonal promos.

linkedin.note: Not recommended.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. Pool services may be LSA-eligible in some markets. Seasonal demand varies.

google.note: Strong local search intent. 'Landscaping near me' drives consistent leads.

meta.note: Excellent for visual showcase — before/after transformations. Great for seasonal promos and recurring maintenance plans.

linkedin.note: Only viable for commercial landscaping contracts targeting property managers.

lsa.note: Strong LSA fit. Moderate CPL with good lead volume in suburban markets. Seasonal demand aligns well.

google.note: Emergency tree removal and trimming searches convert at high rates.

meta.note: Moderate value for storm prep and seasonal trimming campaigns.

linkedin.note: Not recommended.

lsa.note: Strong LSA fit. Affordable CPL and emergency tree removal leads benefit from proximity targeting.

google.note: Strong for residential and commercial painting leads.

meta.note: Before/after photos perform well. Good for color consultation and spring refresh campaigns.

linkedin.note: Only viable for commercial painting contracts targeting property managers and facilities teams.

lsa.note: Strong LSA fit. Moderate CPL with high job values for interior/exterior projects.

google.note: High-intent local searches. Fence installation keywords convert well.

meta.note: Visual platform works well — showcase fence styles and completed projects.

linkedin.note: Not recommended.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. Higher CPL but strong job values. Not as urgency-driven as other LSA categories.

google.note: Strong for driveways, patios, and commercial concrete work.

meta.note: Decorative concrete and stamped concrete photos perform well visually.

linkedin.note: Only for commercial/industrial concrete contractors targeting construction firms.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. Concrete services may be available through LSA in some markets.

google.note: Niche but high-value searches convert well.

meta.note: Highly visual — before/after stamped concrete photos drive strong engagement and leads.

linkedin.note: Not recommended.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. Falls under concrete/masonry LSA categories where available.

google.note: Strong for garage floors and commercial epoxy. High search intent.

meta.note: Transformation photos are very engaging. Metallic epoxy and garage makeovers perform well.

linkedin.note: Some value for commercial/industrial epoxy flooring targeting facilities managers.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. May fall under flooring or concrete LSA categories.

google.note: High-volume search category with strong local intent.

meta.note: Showroom tours, style inspiration, and before/after transformations work well.

linkedin.note: Not recommended for residential flooring.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. Flooring services may be LSA-eligible in some markets.

meta.note: Granite, quartz, and marble showcase photos drive strong engagement.

meta.note: Energy savings messaging and before/after photos work well. Good for seasonal promos.

meta.note: Before/after exterior transformations can drive engagement.

lsa.note: Weak LSA fit. Demolition services are generally not LSA-eligible.

google.note: Emergency searches are almost exclusively on Google. Critical for towing companies.

meta.note: Not effective. Towing is urgency-driven — people don't browse Facebook when they need a tow.

lsa.note: Strong LSA fit. Emergency towing is perfectly suited for proximity-based pay-per-lead targeting.

google.note: High-intent searches like 'movers near me' drive strong leads.

meta.note: Before/after cleanout photos work well. Garage/basement cleanout promos.

google.note: Strong seasonal search intent. High-converting local keywords.

meta.note: Transformation videos and before/after photos are extremely engaging. One of the best visual services for social ads.

google.note: Emergency water/fire damage searches. High urgency, high value.

google.note: Health-urgency searches convert very well.

meta.note: Educational content about mold warning signs can generate awareness leads.

linkedin.note: Good for targeting corporate travel managers and event planners for group bookings and corporate retreats.

linkedin.note: Strong for commercial construction. Target developers, architects, and corporate facilities teams.

lsa.note: Weak LSA fit. General construction is too broad for LSA's service-specific model.

linkedin.note: Limited value for consumer auto sales. Some value for fleet sales targeting fleet managers.

meta.note: Limited social appeal. B2B machining audience isn't on Facebook for this.

linkedin.note: Excellent platform for CNC shops. Target mechanical engineers, procurement managers, and R&D teams sourcing precision machined parts.

google.note: Niche B2B searches for composite manufacturing and repair.

meta.note: Limited social appeal for composite services.

linkedin.note: Good for targeting aerospace, automotive, and marine engineers who need composite manufacturing.

lsa.note: Not recommended. Composite services are B2B and not LSA-eligible.

google.note: B2B searches for custom fabrication services.

meta.note: Limited social appeal.

linkedin.note: Strong for targeting procurement managers, architects, and construction firms sourcing custom fabrication.

lsa.note: Not recommended. Fabrication is a B2B service and not LSA-eligible.

google.note: Good for consulting searches but competitive keywords.

meta.note: Lead gen campaigns and webinar promotions can work. Thought leadership content drives awareness.

linkedin.note: Best platform for consulting firms. Target C-suite executives, business owners, and decision-makers. Thought leadership, case studies, and InMail campaigns drive high-quality leads.

lsa.note: Not recommended. Consulting is a B2B service and not LSA-eligible.

google.note: Strong platform for commercial contracting. Target high-intent searches like "commercial general contractor near me" and "tenant improvement contractor". Good for both local and regional targeting.

meta.note: Limited value for direct lead gen. Use for brand awareness, project showcase content, and retargeting website visitors. Intent is low on social platforms for B2B construction.

linkedin.note: Excellent platform. Target facility managers, property managers, CFOs, and real estate directors. Account-based targeting works especially well for going after known commercial real estate portfolios and property management firms.

lsa.note: Not recommended. Commercial contracting is B2B and not suited for LSA's consumer model.

google.note: Best platform for fleet maintenance. High-intent searches from fleet managers and owner-operators looking for reliable service providers. "Fleet maintenance near me" and "DOT inspection" keywords convert well.

meta.note: Limited value for direct lead gen. Can target fleet managers by job title for awareness, but most fleet repair decisions happen via search, not social browsing.

linkedin.note: Strong for targeting logistics directors, fleet managers, and transportation company executives. Sponsored Content with uptime stats and cost savings resonates well.

lsa.note: Weak LSA fit. Fleet maintenance is B2B-oriented and not suited for LSA's consumer model.

google.note: Excellent platform. Truck repair searches are urgent and high-intent — "truck repair near me", "diesel mechanic", "trailer repair". Strong conversion rates for both owner-operators and fleet managers.

meta.note: Limited. Truck repair is mostly urgency-driven. Some value in showcasing custom fabrication work and building local brand awareness.

linkedin.note: Not ideal for most truck repair services. Only viable for pursuing large fleet maintenance contracts with logistics companies.

lsa.note: Weak LSA fit. Truck repair is not a standard LSA service category.

google.note: Top platform. "RV repair near me", "auto repair", "engine repair" — high-intent searches with strong conversion. Seasonal RV peaks in spring/summer.

meta.note: Strong for RV specifically — RV owners are very active in Facebook groups and communities. Seasonal campaigns, service reminders, and lifestyle content perform well. Auto repair is more moderate.

linkedin.note: Not recommended. RV and auto repair is a consumer service — LinkedIn is not where these customers look for vehicle service.

lsa.note: Moderate LSA fit. Auto repair is LSA-eligible. RV-specific coverage may be limited.

google.note: Best platform for full-service truck and fleet centers. Captures high-intent searches across all service types — truck repair, fleet maintenance, towing, RV service, DOT inspections. Strong conversion from both owner-operators and fleet managers.

meta.note: Moderate value. RV service and custom fabrication showcase well visually. Retargeting website visitors is effective. Not ideal for urgent truck repair or B2B fleet leads.

linkedin.note: Moderate for the fleet/commercial side of the business. Can target logistics directors and fleet managers for maintenance contracts. Not useful for consumer services like RV repair or towing.

lsa.note: Weak LSA fit. Fleet service is B2B-oriented and not suited for LSA's consumer model.

google.note: Google Ads captures high-intent searches for design services. Strong for targeting homeowners actively looking for designers.

meta.note: Interior design is highly visual — Instagram and Facebook are ideal for showcasing portfolio work, before/after transformations, and targeting style-conscious homeowners.

linkedin.note: Limited B2C reach. May work for commercial interior design targeting facility managers or corporate clients.

lsa.note: LSA is available for some design services. Lower competition than trades but also lower search volume for design-specific queries.

google.note: Google Ads captures high-intent searches for architecture firms. Strong for both commercial and residential — people actively search for architects when they have a project.

meta.note: Meta works for showcasing portfolio work and building brand awareness. Better for residential architecture than commercial. Use Instagram for visual portfolio content.

linkedin.note: LinkedIn is strong for commercial architecture — target developers, property managers, corporate real estate directors, and healthcare facility planners.

lsa.note: Not Available. Architecture is not an LSA-eligible service category.

google.note: Excellent. All three service lines (pressure washing, restaurant hood cleaning, lawn care) have high-intent local search traffic. Google Ads is the top-performing channel.

meta.note: Strong for pressure washing and lawn care — before/after visual content performs exceptionally well. Less effective for restaurant hood cleaning (B2B niche); use Meta for residential services primarily.

linkedin.note: Moderate. LinkedIn is effective for commercial contracts — restaurant hood cleaning (target restaurant owners, chain GMs, facility managers who require NFPA 96 compliance) and commercial pressure washing / landscape maintenance contracts. Not for residential work.

lsa.note: Excellent for pressure washing and lawn care — both are LSA-eligible categories with the Google Verified badge. Not available for restaurant hood cleaning (not an LSA category).

google.note: Excellent. High-intent commercial and agricultural electrical searches (e.g. 'grain dryer electrical contractor', '3-phase service upgrade') convert very well. Specialized work with limited competition on specific terms.

meta.note: Moderate. Works for brand awareness and reaching farm operators/equipment owners, but niche B2B audience limits reach. Use alongside Google/LinkedIn — not as primary acquisition.

linkedin.note: Strong for high-value commercial and ag contracts. Target plant managers, facility directors, farm operations managers, ag-coop operations leads, and automation engineers. High CPL offset by large contract values.

lsa.note: Limited. LSA is primarily consumer-oriented for electrical; commercial/industrial/ag work gets fewer leads through LSA. Some benefit for mixed-use (farm shop, home + farm) work only.

google.note: Excellent. Commercial plumbing searches ('commercial plumber near me', 'grease trap service', 'backflow testing') have very high buyer intent. Emergency keywords convert especially well.

meta.note: Moderate. Useful for property manager and restaurant owner awareness campaigns and retargeting. Before/after visuals help, but B2B reach is more limited than consumer plumbing.

linkedin.note: Strong for high-value commercial contracts. Target property managers, facility directors, restaurant operations, healthcare facility managers, and general contractors. High CPL offset by large contract values and recurring revenue.

lsa.note: Moderate. Plumbing is LSA-eligible and the Google Verified badge drives conversion, but most LSA leads are residential/small-business rather than large commercial accounts.

google.note: Effectiveness depends on your specific industry. Google Ads works best when people actively search for your services.

meta.note: Effectiveness depends on your specific industry. Meta works best for visual services and consumer-facing businesses.

linkedin.note: Effectiveness depends on your specific industry. LinkedIn works best for B2B services targeting professionals and decision-makers.

lsa.note: Local Services Ads availability depends on your specific industry. Check Google's LSA eligibility tool for your service type.
